title: aristolochia indica var. lanceolata
intro: Aristolochia indica var. lanceolata, also known as Indian birthwort, is a perennial climbing plant native to the Himalayan region and parts of Asia, characterized by its heart-shaped, lanceolate leaves and distinctive, curved flowers. The plant is traditionally used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Aristolochia indica var. lanceolata are said to include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a diuretic and to lower blood pressure. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of decoctions, infusions, or dried extracts, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it due to potential toxicity and side effects.
